title: Gold Derby accurately predicts 20 of 24 Oscar races in 2026: Analyzing our hits and misses, and the history books
text: Gold Derby achieved an 83% accuracy rate by correctly predicting 20 of 24 Oscar categories at the 2026 ceremony. This performance marks their best result in four years, surpassing the previous three years' accuracy rates of 78%, 74%, and 78% respectively. The platform successfully called all four major acting races: Jessie Buckley (Best Actress), Michael B. Jordan (Best Actor), Amy Madigan (Best Supporting Actress), and Sean Penn (Best Supporting Actor). They also accurately predicted that One Battle After Another would win Best Picture with 77% confidence, extending their streak of correct Best Picture predictions to six consecutive years. Gold Derby's predictions failed in these categories: Best Casting (picked incorrectly for the new category), Best Documentary Feature (selected The Perfect Neighbor instead of Mr. Nobody Against Putin), Best Animated Short (chose Butterfly instead of The Girl Who Cried Pearls), and Best Cinematography for Sinners. Their 2022 performance remains their best on record, achieving 22 of 23 correct predictions. Conversely, 2019 represented their poorest showing with only 14 of 24 accurate calls.
